prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Verily, I doth struggle to comprehend the mystical rhythms of this "cloud-based subscription service", for in the third quarter, their "revenue growth" didst surge like a mighty dragon, increasing by 32% from the preceding annum. Methinks the "customer acquisition costs" didst rise like a foul miasma, consuming 23% of their.getTotal revenue, a sum most fantastical and bewildering to mine eyes. Forgive me, good sirs, but I find it passing strange that their "gross margin" didst remain steadfast, like a noble knight, at 78%, even as the "operating expenses" didst swell like a tempest-tossed sea. In sooth, the "net income" of this mystifying entity didst exceed the prognostications of the wise sages, rising to $12.5 million, a treasure trove of gold and silver that doth defy my understanding of the natural order.
